seo_h1: Italian Cities and the Fragments of Clitonymus

seo.browser_title: Clitonymus the Historian on Eulogikon Greek Archive
seo.meta_title: Clitonymus the Historian Ancient Greek Works Collection
seo.meta_description: Clitonymus the Historian, a 4th century BCE Attic writer. Historical fragments from Classical period. Free to read and download from the Greek library archive.

biography_html:
Clitonymus the Historian was a minor Greek historian of the 4th century BCE. No details of his life or origin survive. He is known only from a single citation in Plutarch, which identifies him as the author of a work on Syracuse.

His lost work, the Syracusan History or On Syracuse, is preserved solely as a title. Plutarch references it as a source for the anecdote of Damon and Phintias, reporting that the tyrant Dionysius I was so impressed by their friendship that he asked to join their bond.

Clitonymus’s significance is minimal and derivative, existing only because Plutarch cited him. His work appears to have been a local history of Syracuse containing anecdotal material. The absence of other citations suggests his history was not widely circulated or influential in antiquity.
